question.text: When market-wide open interest for a scrip exceeds what percentage of MWPL, members can only trade to decrease positions?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Once market-wide OI exceeds 95% of MWPL, members/clients shall trade only to decrease their positions through offsetting positions. This is called the ban period. **The Trap:** The ban period is at 95%, not 100%. It is a preemptive measure before MWPL is fully breached. **Cross-Exam Context:** Ban period questions are commonly asked in NISM VIII as well. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** During ban period, only unwinding trades are permitted - no new position creation is allowed.
